description:
You are an incident responder who has been handed a single SSH log excerpt and asked to quickly identify which source IP addresses are responsible for the largest number of failed interactive-login attempts.

    1. A file containing the raw log snippet already exists at  
       /home/user/logs/auth_sample.log  

    2. Using nothing more sophisticated than the classic text-processing utilities (grep, cut/awk, sort, uniq), analyse that file and produce an **IP frequency report**.

    3. Create a new directory for your findings at  
       /home/user/ir_reports

    4. Inside that directory create a report file named  
       ssh_failed_ip_counts.log

    5. The report **must** follow *exactly* this format so the automated grader can parse it:

       • Line-1  :  # Failed SSH Login Attempts by Source IP  
       • Line-2  :  # Columns: COUNT,IP  
       • Line-3  :  (a single blank line)  
       • Every subsequent line lists a decimal COUNT, a single space, then the dotted-quad IP address that generated that many failures.  
       • The list must be ordered by descending COUNT; ties in COUNT are resolved by ascending (lexicographic) IP address.

       Example layout (numbers and addresses are illustrative only – your script must calculate the real ones from the log):
       -------------------------------------------------
       # Failed SSH Login Attempts by Source IP
       # Columns: COUNT,IP

       27 198.51.100.17
       13 192.168.32.5
       13 203.0.113.9
       2 192.0.2.44
       -------------------------------------------------

    6. Requirements & hints:

       • Only failed password events are relevant; ignore any other lines if they exist.  
       • Your pipeline will typically be 5–10 short commands chained together with pipes and redirects.  
       • Do not hard-code the counts; they must come from processing the file.  
       • Remember that “uniq -c” needs *sorted* input to produce accurate counts.  
       • When everything is finished, the grader will read /home/user/ir_reports/ssh_failed_ip_counts.log and verify both its **path** and **exact textual contents**.

    Once the file is in place and matches the required format, the task is complete.
truth:
Initial files and directories present **before** the agent starts:

    1. Directory: /home/user/logs
    2. File: /home/user/logs/auth_sample.log  
       Contents (10 lines, UNIX line endings):
       ------------------------------------------------------------------
       Jun 10 12:34:56 server sshd[1234]: Failed password for invalid user admin from 192.168.1.10 port 54321 ssh2
       Jun 10 12:35:01 server sshd[1235]: Failed password for root from 203.0.113.5 port 54322 ssh2
       Jun 10 12:35:05 server sshd[1236]: Failed password for invalid user guest from 192.168.1.10 port 54323 ssh2
       Jun 10 12:35:10 server sshd[1237]: Failed password for invalid user test from 198.51.100.25 port 54324 ssh2
       Jun 10 12:35:15 server sshd[1238]: Failed password for root from 203.0.113.5 port 54325 ssh2
       Jun 10 12:35:20 server sshd[1239]: Failed password for invalid user admin from 198.51.100.25 port 54326 ssh2
       Jun 10 12:35:25 server sshd[1240]: Failed password for invalid user admin from 192.168.1.10 port 54327 ssh2
       Jun 10 12:35:30 server sshd[1241]: Failed password for root from 203.0.113.5 port 54328 ssh2
       Jun 10 12:35:35 server sshd[1242]: Failed password for invalid user admin from 192.168.1.10 port 54329 ssh2
       Jun 10 12:35:40 server sshd[1243]: Failed password for invalid user guest from 192.0.2.44 port 54330 ssh2
       ------------------------------------------------------------------

    Nothing else is pre-created.

    Expected artefacts **after** successful task completion:

    1. Directory: /home/user/ir_reports        (permissions 755 or 700 accepted)
    2. File: /home/user/ir_reports/ssh_failed_ip_counts.log  
       EXACT contents that the grader will look for:
       ------------------------------------------------------------------
       # Failed SSH Login Attempts by Source IP
       # Columns: COUNT,IP

       4 192.168.1.10
       3 203.0.113.5
       2 198.51.100.25
       1 192.0.2.44
       ------------------------------------------------------------------

    Notes on verification:

    • Trailing whitespace on each line is **not** allowed.  
    • A single UNIX newline (LF) must follow every line, including the last one.  
    • The file must not contain any extra lines or comments beyond what is specified above.
